Using XMVDecoder::Play

The simplest method of playing an XMV file is to use XMVDecoder::Play. The example below demonstrates all of the steps necessary to play back the file using this method.

  1. You have to create both an IDirect3Device8 and an IDirectSound8 object. 
    IDirectSound8    *pSound  = NULL;
IDirect3D8       *pD3D    = NULL;
IDirect3DDevice8 *pDevice = NULL;

DirectSoundCreate( NULL, &pSound, NULL );

pD3D = Direct3DCreate8( D3D_SDK_VERSION );

// Set up the presentation parameters.
D3DPRESENT_PARAMETERS d3dPP;
ZeroMemory( &d3dPP, sizeof( D3DPRESENT_PARAMETERS ) );

d3dPP.BackBufferWidth                 = 640;
d3dPP.BackBufferHeight                = 480;
d3dPP.BackBufferFormat                = D3DFMT_LIN_A8R8G8B8;
d3dPP.BackBufferCount                 = 1;
d3dPP.SwapEffect                      = D3DSWAPEFFECT_DISCARD;
d3dPP.FullScreen_RefreshRateInHz      = 60;
d3dPP.FullScreen_PresentationInterval = D3DPRESENT_INTERVAL_ONE;
d3dPP.EnableAutoDepthStencil          = TRUE;
d3dPP.AutoDepthStencilFormat          = D3DFMT_D24S8;

pD3D->CreateDevice( D3DADAPTER_DEFAULT, 
                    D3DDEVTYPE_HAL,
                    0,
                    D3DCREATE_HARDWARE_VERTEXPROCESSING,
                    &d3dPP,
                    &pDevice );
  2. Next, you must create the decoder and load the XMV file using XMVDecoder_CreateDecoderForFile. 
    XMVDecoder *pDecoder = NULL;

XMVDecoder_CreateDecoderForFile( XMVFLAG_FULL_LOOP, 
                                 "d:\media\MyVideo.xmv", 
                                 &pDecoder );
  3. In order to play back audio, you need to enable at least one audio stream. While it is possible to embed multiple audio streams into an XMV file, typically you want to play only one at at time. If you are trying to enable multiple streams, you can get the number of audio streams in the XMV file by calling XMVDecoder::GetVideoDescriptor. This example enables only the first stream by calling XMVDecoder::EnableAudioStream. 
    pDecoder->EnableAudioStream(i, 0, NULL, NULL );
  4. Either IDirect3DDevice8::Present or IDirect3DDevice8::Swap must be called at least once after the device is created in order for the video mode to be set correctly. If no rendering has occured, you will need to call it explictly. 
    pDevice->Clear( 0, NULL, 0, D3DCLEAR_TARGET, 0, 0.0, 0 );
pDevice->Swap( D3DSWAP_DEFAULT );
  5. Finally, start the playback using XMVDecoder::Play. 
    pDecoder->Play( XMVFLAG_FULL_LOOP, NULL );

    XMV files that are creating using XMVFLAG_FULL_LOOP will block the thread that they are playing on until either XMVDecoder::TerminateLoop or XMVDecoder::TerminatePlayback is called.
